data.title: Hong Kong Shares Rise, Tracking U.S. Stock Futures — Market Talk
data.description: Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index closed 1.4% higher at 20891.62, tracking U.S. futures. With some easing of anxiety about U.S. tariffs, market attention is redirected to U.S. economic data releases this week, which will provide more clues about the health of the economy and the Fed's next move, ANZ Research writes in a note. BYD jumped 11.5% after the company said it will host a press conference next week about its smart-car strategy. Sunny Optical Technology gained 9.9% and SMIC was 7.2% higher. Among decliners, WH Group lost 1.6%, Baidu fell 1.3% and Galaxy Entertainment was 1.0% lower. The Hang Seng Tech Index rose 2.6% to end at 5059.38, a 2025 high. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Helix Energy Solutions Unit Wins Trenching Services Contract Offshore UK
data.description:
Helix Energy Solutions Group said late Wednesday its Helix Robotics Solutions subsidiary won a contract from Seaway7 for the burial of inter-array cables for Orsted's Hornsea 3 offshore windfarm project located off the UK's Norfolk coast.

The project scope includes the burial of cables totaling about 500 kilometers in length, the company said.

The contract is anticipated to begin in Q3 2026 and is expected to last over 300 days.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0843 GMT - Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index closed 1.4% higher at 20891.62, tracking U.S. futures. With some easing of anxiety about U.S. tariffs, market attention is redirected to U.S. economic data releases this week, which will provide more clues about the health of the economy and the Fed's next move, ANZ Research writes in a note. BYD jumped 11.5% after the company said it will host a press conference next week about its smart-car strategy. Sunny Optical Technology gained 9.9% and SMIC was 7.2% higher. Among decliners, WH Group lost 1.6%, Baidu fell 1.3% and Galaxy Entertainment was 1.0% lower. The Hang Seng Tech Index rose 2.6% to end at 5059.38, a 2025 high. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0839 GMT - Compass Group has a high-quality earnings stream and growth prospects despite an uncertain environment, Shore Capital analyst Greg Johnson writes in a note. The London-listed catering contractor's first-quarter organic sales growth was slightly above forecast and ahead of full-year assumptions, Johnson says. Pricing and volumes will likely ease through the year, with like-for-like volumes a potential positive driver to forecasts, he adds. Shares are down 1.9% at 2,737 pence.(najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0837 GMT - WeChat's e-commerce system could gain stronger awareness among consumers in 2025 through a broader adoption of its gifting function, Citi analysts say in a research note. The Tencent-owned popular messaging app in December rolled out a gifting function, which has successfully expanded the consumption scenarios within the app's ecosystem during the Lunar New Year, the analysts note. Citi predicts the function to be more widely used, thanks to the app's "social graph" alongside a growing number of participating merchants. Citi maintains a buy rating on Tencent, with its target price unchanged at HK$563.00. Shares of Tencent last ended at HK$420.40. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0836 GMT - Carlsberg's 2024 performance and targets for 2025 are welcomed considering the challenging backdrop in the beverage sector, RBC Capital Markets analysts James Edwardes Jones and Wassachon Udomsilpa say in a note. Despite the ongoing weak consumer sentiment and the supply-chain issues, the Danish brewer's performance in Western Europe was slightly better than expected, they say. Performance in China declined 4% as it continued to be affected by a weak consumer backdrop. However, this is consistent with sector reports, the analysts add. For 2025, Carlsberg expects a relatively stable consumer environment despite ongoing uncertainty in both Asia and Europe, they say. Shares are up 3.1% at 758.60 Danish kronor. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

0824 GMT - Gucci's potential turnaround is likely to be pushed further into 2026, after parent company Kering decided to part ways with the brand's creative director Sabato De Sarno, RBC Capital Markets analysts Piral Dadhania and Richard Chamberlain write in a research note. The French luxury giant said it would announce a new artistic direction for its flagship label in due time. The termination of the collaboration doesn't come as a surprise, given Gucci's underperformance compared with peers since De Sarno's appointment, they say. A new creative direction could be the necessary next step for Gucci to recover some of the lost market share, the analysts add. Shares are down 2.7% and have dropped around 39% in the past 12 months. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0829 GMT - Pernod Ricard's guidance cut means the company is adopting a more realistic framework for its outlook that could pave the way for a share recovery down the line, Jefferies's Edward Mundy and Andrei Andon-Ionita say. The maker of Absolut vodka and Chivas Scotch whisky now forecasts a fall in organic sales for fiscal 2025, against its previous expectations of a return to growth. Consensus expectations had already moved below the company's guidance and shares in Pernod Ricard had been weak on concerns about its performance in China, the analysts say in a research note. The guidance cut should reduce the risk for Pernod Ricard's full-year earnings disappointing, Jefferies says. Shares rise 2.4%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0826 GMT - Watches of Switzerland Group's maintained guidance will likely be reassuring, Shore Capital's David Hughes writes. The London-based luxury watch retailer's third-quarter update shows encouraging signs for sales, Hughes says in a note. These include the positive performance of its businesses, improving market conditions in U.K. and continued momentum in the U.S. "Given the worsening views on the economic outlook of the U.K., and a number of recent profit downgrades from retailers following a softer seasonal period, a report of good trading and maintained fiscal 2025 guidance should reassure," he adds. Shares are down 1.8% at 544.50 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0822 GMT - Max Financial Services' life-insurance unit seems well placed to achieve its FY 2025 guidance, Nomura analysts say in a research report. For the unit, management guides for 20% annual-premium-equivalent growth and high-single-digit growth for value of new business in FY 2025, the analysts note. Management appears reasonably confident that the unit can post a VNB margin of 23%-24% in FY 2025, the analysts say. With the effects of India's surrender value regulations for the insurance industry now mostly factored in, the company appears well on course to deliver on the guidance, the analysts add. Nomura upgrades the stock to buy from neutral and raises the target price to INR1,300.00 from INR1,250.00. Shares are 1.0% lower at INR1,107.60. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0810 GMT - Hannover Re's confirmation of its guidance for 2025 is positive in light of the Los Angeles wildfires, which have been a clear drag on the reinsurance sector year to date, analysts at JPMorgan say. The German reinsurer didn't provide an estimate on the impact of the California fires, but it did reaffirm its previous guidance for net profit of 2.4 billion euros this year. It also confirmed earnings for last year at 2.3 billion euros, in line with previous guidance and said it achieved premium growth of 7.6% at its January treaty renewals with pricing reduced 2.1% on a risk-adjusted basis. "Price reductions for Hannover Re take profitability back to 2023 levels which was a strong baseline in our view," JPM says. Shares rise 0.3%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0803 GMT - U.S. tariffs will likely have a minimal impact on software companies, while hardware companies will face a bigger hit, Janus Henderson Investors says in a commentary. Software isn't reliant on physical asset production and has a geographical distributed workforce, whereas hardware is often manufactured abroad and relies on components such as semiconductor chips and wafers. The components are mainly produced in Asia-Pacific and then shipped to Mexico for assembly to take advantage of lower labor costs, the asset manager adds. "The impact of the recently announced tariffs would therefore be most pronounced in the assembly phase." Janus Henderson expects the impact on large end-market manufacturers like Dell and HP to be more acute than their suppliers. (farah.elias@wsj.com)

0750 GMT - China's equity markets could have a tough year regardless of how the trade tensions play out, Capital Economics analyst Thomas Mathews says in a research note. Investors may be overly optimistic about the likelihood of a reprieve on tariffs, the analyst says, and tariffs are not the only reason to be downbeat. The prospects of further fiscal stimulus, which investors have put high hopes on, are uncertain, he says. "A separate problem is that Chinese companies have struggled for a long time to generate sustained growth in earnings per share," he adds. Therefore, there's play of room for conditions in China's markets to deteriorate if the tariffs remain in place, he adds.(sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0726 GMT - Japanese stocks ended higher amid receding concerns of trade friction between the U.S. and its trading partners. Brokerage and electronics stocks led the gains. Nomura Holdings rose 3.8% after its nine-month net profit more than doubled. Renesas Electronics surged 13% after its 4Q results. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.6% to 39066.53. USD/JPY is at 152.64, compared with 152.60 as of Wednesday 5 p.m. Eastern time. Investors are closely watching for developments regarding U.S. tariffs. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield fell 2 basis points to 1.260%. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: COMMENT-Inflation data a double-edged sword for the crown
data.description:
A significant rise in Swedish inflation has ramifications for monetary policy, the crown and economic growth. A steady interest rate stance is SEK supportive, but detrimental to Swedish growth and could lean on investor sentiment.

The jump in Swedish inflation in January strongly suggests the Riksbank's easing cycle might be over and the initial market reaction has been to maintain the crown's recovery versus the euro.

The Riksbank's 2.0% CPIF inflation target has been exceeded. The flash number for January is clear above the central bank's 1.8% estimate at 2.2% y/y, and significantly higher than the Reuters poll consensus of 1.6%. Excluding energy, CPIF rose 2.7% y/y, again above the Riksbank's call and up from December's 2.0% print.

The data provided is only a preliminary release, lacking details on the key drivers of the index. Despite this, a rate cut in March can be ruled out and our forecast for a cut in May now appears increasingly uncertain.

EUR/SEK is set for a fourth consecutive bear session and eyeing the 11.2970 low from October. A trending market could also target the double day 11.2450 low from September, but progress beyond this support point might be slowed if the Swedish economy begins to show signs of stress and inflation becomes entrenched above 2.0%.

For more click on

data.title: Prism Johnson Re-Appoints Vijay Aggarwal As Managing Director
data.description:
Prism Johnson Ltd :

  • PRISM JOHNSON LTD - RE-APPOINTS VIJAY AGGARWAL AS MANAGING DIRECTOR

  • PRISM JOHNSON LTD - RE-APPOINTS SARAT KUMAR CHANDAK AS EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR & CEO

data.title: German Construction Activity Downturn Continues at Start of 2025
data.description:
Germany's construction sector continued to post a slump in activity in January amid sustained headwinds to demand, Hamburg Commercial Bank and S&P Global said Thursday.

The HCOB Germany Construction PMI Total Activity Index reached a 20-month high of 42.5 in January, up from 37.8 in the prior month.

data.title: Tosoh's Nine-Month Profit Grows 17%
data.description:
Tosoh recorded a 17.3% rise in attributable profit for the nine months ended Dec. 31, 2024, to 48.5 billion yen from 41.4 billion yen in the year-ago period, a Thursday bourse filing said.

Earnings per share were 152.36 yen, up from 129.93 yen in the corresponding period of the previous fiscal year.

The chemical and specialty materials company saw a 6.4% rise in net sales to 795.7 billion yen in the period from 747.9 billion yen a year prior.

The company expects an attributable profit of 61 billion yen, basic EPS of 191.55 yen, net sales of 1.07 trillion yen, and a total dividend of 100 yen per share for the fiscal year ending March 31.

Shares of the company closed nearly 5% higher on Thursday.

data.title: Zhejiang Development Group Issues Two Short Ultra Short-Term Bonds Worth 9 Billion Yuan
data.description:
Zhejiang Development Group issued its first and second batches of ultra-short-term financing bonds for 2025, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

The first issuance raised 5 billion yuan with a 2.41% interest rate for 148 days, while the second raised 4 billion yuan with a 2.32% for 265 days.

Ningbo Bank and Bohai Bank served as lead underwriters. These issuances are part of a larger 22.5 billion yuan bond program to enhance liquidity and financial flexibility.

data.title: India's Prism Johnson posts profit after four straight quarterly losses on tax gains
data.description:
Indian construction materials maker Prism Johnson reported a profit for the first time in five quarters on Thursday, helped by tax gains related to previous periods.

The company reported a net profit of 481.2 million rupees ($5.5 million), during the October-December period, compared with a loss of 34.5 million rupees a year ago.

Shares of the Hyderabad-based company spiked up to 8% after the results, before losing all of its gains.

However, at a pre-tax basis, the company's net loss widened to 337.1 million rupees, from 89.7 million rupees a year earlier.

Net sales rose 6% on strong performance in its insurance and tilemaking segments, which cushioned weakness in cement and ready-made concrete businesses.

Cement companies have so far reported a mixed set of earnings in the quarter, where they grappled with soft demand and weak prices.

Strong volumes boosted market leader UltraTech , its rival Adani Group's cement businesses Ambuja and ACC , while smaller company Dalmia Bharat took a hit in the quarter due to lower prices.

Cement business, which formed around 39% of the topline, saw a 2.5% drop in sales. Revenue from ready-made concrete businesses also saw a fall of 6.5%.

However, other businesses helped offset the impact. The tilemaking segment rose 4.8%, while insurance business surged over threefold.

($1 = 87.5370 Indian rupees)

data.title: PMI: Italian Construction Sector Growth Softens in January
data.description:
Growth in Italy's construction sector continued at the beginning of 2025, albeit at a softer rate than the previous month, thanks to residential building activity, a survey showed Thursday.

The HCOB Italy Construction Purchasing Managers' Index edged down to 50.9 in January from 51.2 in the prior month, according to Hamburg Commercial Bank and S&P Global.

data.title: Spain's stock market regulator halts trading in Talgo shares
data.description:
Spanish stock market regulator CNMV suspended trading in shares of train maker Talgo on Thursday after several media reports that the Basque steel company Sidenor is preparing a takeover.

Sidenor opened talks about the possible acquisition of a stake in Talgo in October.

Newspaper Cinco Dias said Sidenor was planning to offer up to 4.80 euros ($4.98) per share for a 29.9% stake in Talgo that is currently held by investment fund Trilantic.

Sidenor declined to comment. Talgo and Trilantic did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

The Spanish government in August blocked a 5 euro per share offer made by Hungarian consortium Ganz-Mavag for Talgo.

($1 = 0.9644 euros)

data.title: Phoenix Motor Announces New Autonomous Vehicle Development for Food and Beverage Delivery
data.description:
ANAHEIM, CA / ACCESS Newswire / February 6, 2025 / Phoenix Motor Inc. , a leading manufacturer of heavy-duty transit buses and electrification solutions provider for medium-duty vehicles, today announced a strategic initiative to develop autonomous electric vehicles tailored for the food and beverage delivery industry. This initiative will commence with a pilot project in collaboration with a Silicon Valley-based food delivery service company in California.

As part of this partnership, Phoenix Motor will design and manufacture a fleet of autonomous food delivery electric vehicles (EVs) to meet the increasing demand for last-mile delivery solutions. The initial phase of the project includes the production and deployment of ten autonomous delivery vehicles as part of a trial program, with plans for future expansion based on performance and market demand.

"Phoenix Motor is excited to enter the autonomous food and beverage delivery space, leveraging our expertise in commercial EV technology to create a next-generation solution for urban logistics," said Denton Peng, CEO of Phoenix Motor. "With autonomous technology expected to transform the transportation industry, this collaboration represents a significant milestone in our commitment to innovation and sustainability."

The new autonomous delivery vehicles will integrate advanced self-driving capabilities to optimize route efficiency, reduce operational costs, and enhance the overall consumer experience. This initiative aligns with Phoenix Motor's broader vision of providing cutting-edge mobility solutions that drive efficiency and sustainability in the commercial transportation sector.

The company will work closely with its Silicon Valley-based partner to refine vehicle specifications, software integration, and operational logistics to ensure seamless performance in real-world delivery applications. Phoenix Motor will manufacture the vehicles, while its partner will define specific operational requirements to meet the evolving needs of the food and beverage delivery market.

About Phoenix Motor Inc.

Phoenix Motor, a pioneer in the electric vehicle ("EV") industry, designs, builds, and integrates electric drive systems and manufactures heavy duty transit buses and medium and light duty commercial EVs. Phoenix operates two primary brands, "Phoenix", which is focused on commercial products including heavy and medium duty EVs (transit buses, shuttle buses, school buses and delivery trucks, among others) and "EdisonFuture", which intends to offer light-duty EVs. Phoenix endeavors to be a leading designer, developer and manufacturer of electric vehicles and electric vehicle technologies. To learn more, please visit: phoenixev.ai.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains forward-looking statements, as that term is defined in the Private Litigation Reform Act of 1995, that involve significant risks and uncertainties. Forward-looking statements can be identified through the use of words such as "may," "might," "will," "intend," "should," "could," "can," "would," "continue," "expect," "believe," "anticipate," "estimate," "predict," "outlook," "potential," "plan," "seek," and similar expressions and variations or the negatives of these terms or other comparable terminology. Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements, which reflect the Company's current expectations and speak only as of the date of this release. Actual results may differ materially from the Company's current expectations depending upon a number of factors. These risk factors include, among others, those related to our ability to raise additional capital necessary to grow our business, operations and business and financial performance, our ability to grow demand for our products and revenue, our ability to become profitable, our ability to have access to an adequate supply of parts and materials and other critical components for our vehicles on the timeline we expect, the coronavirus (COVID-19) and the effects of the outbreak and actions taken in connection therewith, adverse changes in general economic and market conditions, competitive factors including but not limited to pricing pressures and new product introductions, uncertainty of customer acceptance of new product offerings and market changes, risks associated with managing the growth of the business, and those other risks and uncertainties that are described in the "Risk Factors" section of the Company's annual report filed on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Except as required by law, the Company does not undertake any responsibility to revise or update any forward-looking statements.

Contact:

ir@phoenixev.ai

Dave Gentry, CEO

RedChip Companies, Inc.

1-407-644-4256

PEV@redchip.com

data.title: ArcelorMittal Q4 Adjusted Earnings Fall, Sales Rise
data.description:
ArcelorMittal reported Q4 adjusted earnings Thursday of $0.52 per share, down from $1.18 a year earlier.

A single analyst polled by FactSet expected $0.59.

Sales for the quarter ended Dec. 31 were $14.71 billion, up from $14.55 billion a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $15.03 billion.

data.title: New China Life Insurance: He Xingda Resigned as Nonexecutive Director
Zoujaj Starts Commercial Production of New Glass Container Line
data.description:
The National Co. for Glass Industries (SASE:2150), d/b/a Zoujaj, began commercial production of its new glass container line.

The sixth production line boosts the Saudi Arabian glass container manufacturer's annual production capacity by 25,000 tons, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Italian Construction Sector Starts 2025 with Modest Growth
data.description: The HCOB Italy Construction PMI edged down slightly to 50.9 in January 2025 from 51.2 in December 2024, marking the second consecutive month of expansion in the sector.

data.title: BioVersys Concludes CHF80 Million Swiss IPO
data.description:
BioVersys completed its initial public offering on the SIX Swiss Exchange, securing 80 million francs in gross proceeds, according to a Thursday release.

The biopharmaceutical company issued 2,083,333 new shares at 36 francs apiece, implying a market capitalization of 212.9 million francs.

The majority of the IPO proceeds will be used to further advance the group's lead asset BV100, while a portion is allotted for the clinical development of its second lead asset alpibectir with GSK . The remaining amount is intended for its preclinical pipeline.

BioVersys shares will commence trading on Friday under the ticker symbol 'BIOV'.

data.title: European Stocks Gain for 3rd Session, Earnings Eyed
data.description:
The STOXX 50 gained 0.4% and the STOXX 600 added 0.5% on Thursday, marking a third consecutive session of gains, boosted by strong corporate results.

Shares of AstraZeneca gained almost 4% after the company's earnings and revenue topped forecasts and the pharmaceutical giant said it expects further sales.

Societe Generale jumped about 7% after reporting a rise in Q4 revenue and saying it is targeting revenue growth and higher profitability for the year.

Maersk soared more than 6% after the company started a $2 billion share buyback program.

On the other hand, Volvo sank more than 7% after warning of a challenging 2025 and Kering fell 2.8% after announcing that Gucci creative director Sabato de Sarno is leaving after only two years.

Meanwhile, traders continue to monitor developments in the trade war.

data.title: Market Chatter: South Korea to Monitor China's Strategic Mineral Export Controls Amid US Trade Dispute
data.description:
South Korea's Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy plans to closely monitor and respond to the potential impacts of China's export restrictions on key strategic minerals, Yonhap News reported Wednesday.

The move comes after Beijing imposed at least a 10% tariff on American imports in retaliation for US tariffs, the report said.

China also unveiled export controls on five critical metals — tungsten, indium, bismuth, tellurium, and molybdenum — essential for semiconductors and display manufacturing, it said.

South Korea currently holds tungsten reserves for six months and molybdenum for three months. China supplies over 85% of South Korea's tungsten and more than 90 percent of its molybdenum, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: AstraZeneca tops FTSE 100 on robust Q4 results and 2025 forecast
data.description:
** Shares of AstraZeneca up as much as 4.6% to 11,636p; hitting their highest since October 2024

** Co top pct gainer on FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index

** Drugmaker logs Q4 core EPS and revenue just above company-compiled analysts' estimate

** Reports Q4 total revenue at $14.9 billion vs estimate of $14.2 billion

** Q4 core EPS of $2.09 above estimate of $2.07

** Co expects 2025 revenue to increase by a high single-digit percentage, with core earnings to grow in low double-digit percentage

** Analysts expect 2025 sales growth of 6.5% and profit increase of 12.6%, according to data compiled by LSEG

** "Astra looks well on the way to achieving its $80bn revenue target by 2030, but there may be some disappointment that this bar hasn't been raised" - Hargreaves Lansdown analyst

** Stock up about 10% over last 12 months

data.title: BioStock: Double celebration for Lipum
data.description:
Chordate Medical

Biotech company Lipum has two reasons to celebrate in 2025: not only its 15th anniversary but also the 80th birthday of its co-founder, Professor Olle Hernell. In an interview with BioStock, Hernell reflects on Lipum’s journey – from its academic research origins to becoming a publicly traded company developing a novel biologic drug.

Read the full article at biostock.se:

https://www.biostock.se/en/2025/02/double-celebration-for-lipum/

This is a press release from BioStock - Connecting Innovation & Capital. https://www.biostock.se

https://news.cision.com/chordate-medical/r/biostock-double-celebration-for-lipum%2Cc4101658

data.title: China, Hong Kong stocks rise as DeepSeek AI optimism lifts tech sector
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks advanced on Thursday, driven by the tech sector, as investors continued to bet on domestic artificial intelligence firms following Chinese AI start-up DeepSeek's breakthrough.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 index was up 1.26% at the close, and the Shanghai Composite index rose 1.27% to 3,270.66 points, a five-week high.

** The Hang Seng Index in Hong Kong closed up 1.43% at 20,891.62, a three-month high.

** AI-related sectors led gains in both onshore and offshore markets. The CSI Semiconductor Industry Index (.CSI931865) surged 5.08%, and the Hang Seng Tech Index climbed 2.62% to the highest level since last October.

** Shares of chipmaker Naura surged 8.8%, their biggest single-day advance in nearly three months, while SMIC's Hong Kong-listed shares rallied nearly 7.16% to a record high.

** Enthusiasm for domestic AI-related firms showed no signs of abating after DeepSeek disrupted the global industry last month with advanced models at low cost.

** "DeepSeek's breakthrough is driving a renewed confidence in China's AI sector and it will continue to be a key market theme," analysts at China Securities said in a note.

** It is challenging the prevailing notion that China lags several years behind U.S. competitors in the AI race, and the sector could chart its own course in a revaluation despite global volatility, they added.

** Around the region, MSCI's Asia ex-Japan stock index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) was firmer by 0.44% while Japan's Nikkei index was up 0.61%.

data.title: Eurozone Construction Slump Eases in January, PMI Data Shows
data.description:
The euro area's construction sector remained in contraction territory in January, although the rate of decline in total activity was the slowest in nearly two years.

The HCOB Eurozone Construction PMI Total Activity Index rose to 45.4 from 42.9, Hamburg Commercial Bank and S&P Global said Thursday. The reading indicates the softest contraction since February 2023.

data.title: Compass Group's Growth Prospects Look Good — Market Talk
data.description: Compass Group has a high-quality earnings stream and growth prospects despite an uncertain environment, Shore Capital analyst Greg Johnson writes in a note. The London-listed catering contractor's first-quarter organic sales growth was slightly above forecast and ahead of full-year assumptions, Johnson says. Pricing and volumes will likely ease through the year, with like-for-like volumes a potential positive driver to forecasts, he adds. Shares are down 1.9% at 2,737 pence.(najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Finnish Trade Deficit Narrows in December
data.description:
Finland’s trade deficit narrowed to EUR 0.11 billion in December 2024 from EUR 0.52 billion from the corresponding month of the previous year, preliminary estimates showed.

Imports dropped by 1.6% year-on-year to EUR 6.13 billion, due to reduced purchases from non-EU (-9.7%) although it improved for EU countries (5.5%).

Imports fell mostly from United Kingdom (-37.1%), China (-25.5%), Switzerland (-14.0%), and Sweden (-8.5%).

Meanwhile, exports increased by 5.5% to EUR 6.02 billion, boosted by sales to EU (7.2%) and non-EU countries (3.1%).

Among trading partners, exports improved mainly to Latvia (207.7%), US (36.0%), Sweden (27.6%), and Canada (24.5%).

For the whole year of 2024, the country recorded a trade deficit of EUR 2.12 billion.

data.title: Fondia Oyj - Managers' Transactions - Timo Lappi/Placereri AB
data.description:
Fondia

Fondia Plc

Managers’ Transactions

2025-02-06 at 10.32 a.m. EET

There has been a merger in the chain of ownership of the shares controlled by Timo Lappi. Placereri AB, the former owner of Fondia Plc's shares, has merged into Sijoittamo Oy. As a result of the merger, Placereri AB has been dissolved and its assets and liabilities, including its shares in Fondia Plc, have been transferred to Sijoittamo Oy.

Timo Lappi, Interim CEO; Chair of the Board of Directors, tel. +358 40 756 7809  

Fondia in brief

Fondia solves the legal needs of companies by combining the best services from internal legal departments and law firms. Fondia operates in Finland, Sweden, Estonia, and Lithuania, and the Group’s net sales in 2023 were EUR 26.1 million. Fondia employs around 190 people.

data.title: Uno Minda Gets Board Nod to Raise Up To INR5 Billion Through Debenture Issue
data.description:
Auto components manufacturer Uno Minda has received board approval to raise up to 5 billion Indian rupees through the issue of non-convertible debentures in one or more debentures, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The funds will be used to meet the fund requirement for capital expenditure and investments in subsidiaries, joint ventures, and associate companies of the Uno Minda.

data.title: Booz Allen Hamilton Says Cisco Completes Acquisition of SnapAttack
data.description:
Booz Allen Hamilton said late Wednesday that Cisco has completed its acquisition of SnapAttack, the cyber-security firm that was spun out of Booz Allen in 2021 and in which it continued to retain an investment.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

SnapAttack was incubated and launched publicly by Booz Allen Hamilton's DarkLabs cyber division, the company said.
